Two stacked London Marathon elite fields have left pundits divided as to who to back in 2026, with records threatening to be broken on what promises to be a sunny Sunday morning in the U.K. capital.

Read on and we'll show you how to watch London Marathon live streams from anywhere with a VPN — and potentially for FREE.

Kenya's Sabastian Sawe is among this year's bookies' favorites in the men's race, having taken the win last year. Uganda's Jacob Kiplimo was the 2025 runner-up in his London Marathon debut, so will be eager to go one better this time around. Ethiopia haven't had a male winner here for half a decade, an anomaly that the likes of Deresa Geleta and Olympic champion Tamirat Tola will hope to redress.

Having set the world record last year for the women's-only race, crossing the line in 2 hours, 15 minutes, 50 seconds, Ethiopian Tigst Assefa is back to defend her crown. Kenya's Joyciline JepkosgeiIt — the 2021 victor — looks like the woman most likely to disrupt the first back-to-back in six years, while Hellen Obiri of Kenya and Julia Paternain of Uruguay have also won significant marathon medals recently. It's just a pity that both Peres Jepchirchir and Sifan Hassan have had to withdraw in the lead-up to Sunday's race,

Aside from the elite competitors, the much-loved mass participation event is set to feature an array of famous faces, with the likes of "Wicked" actor Cynthia Erivo, former F1 driver Sebastian Vettel and Olympians Dame Laura Kenny and Sir Ben Ainslie all running. news.title: How to watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026: FREE live streams, TV channels and preview for the cycling race
news.text:
Watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 free streams to potentially see a battle of the ages between World Champion Tadej Pogačar, two-time winner Remco Evenepoel and new sensation Paul Seixas on the vicious Wallonian climbs.

With 11 evil climbs starting with the Côte de Saint-Roch at 83km and ending with the Côte de la Roche-aux-Faucons with 13km left before the finish you just have to love hills to win Liège-Bastogne-Liège. With many of the ascents featuring ramps up over 20% this race is one for the true lightweights, the mountain goats of the professional peloton.

Scroll down the page for our full preview, including the all-important course information.

You can watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 live streams from anywhere with a VPN, and potentially for FREE.

Watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 live streams for free

Free Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 streaming is available in Australia, France, Belgium and the Netherlands:

Abroad? If you're usually based in any of these countries but aren't at home this weekend, don't worry — you can unblock your local stream with a VPN. We'll show you how to do that below.

Watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 streams from abroad

Away from home at the moment and blocked from watching the cycling on your usual subscription service?

You can still watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 live streams thanks to the wonders of a VPN (Virtual Private Network). The software enables your devices to appear to be back in your home country regardless of where in the world you are. So, it's ideal for sports fans away on vacation or on business. Our favorite is NordVPN. It's the best on the market:

Using a VPN is incredibly simple.

1. Install the VPN of your choice. As we've said, NordVPN is our favorite.

2. Choose the location you wish to connect to in the VPN app. For instance if you're in the U.S. and want to view an Australian service, you'd select Australia from the list.

3. Sit back and enjoy the action. Head to SBS OnDemand (or another streaming service) and watch the racing.

How to watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 live streams in the U.S.

The race will be hosted in the USA by NBCUniversal’s paid-for streaming service, Peacock.

You need the Peacock Premium package to watch live sport. Prices start from $10.99 per month, though you can save money if you sign up for the year — $109.99 up-front works out at 12 months for the price of 10.

If you're out of the US but still want to watch the 2026 Liège-Bastogne-Liège on Peacock then don't forget to explore the VPN route set out above, which will help you access your subscriptions from anywhere.

How to watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 live streams in the U.K.

Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 is on TNT Sports in the UK.

TNT Sports’ cycling coverage in the UK has now moved from Discovery+ to the HBO Max platform. It costs £30.99 per month, though there is a better value £25.99 "saver plan" available if you sign up for a 12-month term.

If you already had a Discovery+ account, it will be automatically transferred over to HBO Max.

Coverage of Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 starts at 11:30 a.m. BST.

If you're currently traveling overseas, don't worry, as you can use NordVPN to watch your usual service from abroad.

How to watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 live streams in Canada

Fans in Canada can watch the Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 on FloBikes.

A subscription to Flobikes, which has pretty much every race you could wish to watch, costs $49.99 a month or $215.88 for the year.

Not at home right now? Use NordVPN or another VPN service to trick your device into thinking you're still in Canada.

How to watch Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 live streams in Australia.

In Australia? As previously mentioned, you can watch the Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 for free on SBS on Demand.

Not at home right now? Use NordVPN or another VPN service to trick your device into thinking you're still in Australia.

Liège-Bastogne-Liège 2026 Preview

Liège-Bastogne-Liège is the oldest and the last of the spring Monuments, a savage race set on a 260km course in Wallonia, the French speaking part of Belgium. This year the 112th edition of the race features 3,700 meters of climbing with the final ascent, the Côte de la Roche-aux-Faucons, coming just 13 kilometers from the finish line.

Packed with famous climbs such as the Côte de Saint-Roch, Côte de Wanne, Côte de Stockeu and the brutal Côte de la Redoute, it wears down the riders, leaving only the very strongest able to fight for the win.

Leading the list of favorites is once more Tadej Pogačar who is aiming for his fourth win to place him one shy of the record five set by Eddy Merckx. After missing out at Paris-Roubaix, Pog will be keen to get his season ‘back on track’ and add to his already huge tally of Monuments with another win here. But can anyone stop him?

Among those lining up in the vain hope he has an off day are two-time winner Remco Evenepoel, who must believe he has a chance, and new French sensation Paul Sexias who goes head-to-head with Pogačar for the first time this year.

In what could be the first episode of a rivalry for the ages it will be fascinating to see how the 19-year-old wonder kid fairs against the best rider of this, and possibly any, generation.

The U.K.’s Tom Pidcock is also set to ride, having just won a stage at the Tour of the Alps less than a month after his crash into a ravine at the Volta a Catalunya.

news.title: Hurdle hints and answers for April 26, 2026
news.text:
If you like playing daily word games like Wordle, then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown.An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. news.title: Horoscope for Sunday, April 26, 2026
news.text:
There are no restrictions to shopping or important decisions today. The moon is in Virgo.

Aries (March 21-April 19)

 This is a gorgeous day to enjoy socializing with others. Romance is in the air, but more than that, you’ll also enjoy the beauty of your daily surroundings. Make an effort to connect with others. Conversations with siblings, relatives and neighbors will be warm and friendly.

Taurus (April 20-May 20)

 This is a feel-good, romantic day! Some might choose to express their affection for someone by buying them a gift. In fact, you have an ability to attract money and gifts as well today. (Keep your pockets open.) Things will go your way with the sun in your sign!

Gemini (May 21-June 20)

 This is a dreamy, pleasant day for you because fair Venus is in your sign flirting with idealistic Neptune, making you want to be with people who are compassionate and tender. You feel unselfish and spiritual. Romantic relationships will feel like you have found your soulmate.

Cancer (June 21-July 22)

 Your idealism is aroused today. This is also a popular time for you because you’re more involved with others. You might find it rewarding to work with a charitable organization or do what you can to help those who are less fortunate. With Mercury high in your chart, people will listen.

Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)

 You feel warmhearted and friendly to others today. For many, a friendship might turn romantic or have a hint of flirtation because romantic vibes are in the air. Meanwhile, remember that you look fabulous to everyone because the sun is high in your chart, casting you in a flattering spotlight.

Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)

 Today the moon is in your sign, which heightens your feelings and perhaps makes you want to use your influence (directly or indirectly) to help someone. Some of you might also develop a crush on your boss or someone in a position of authority. Affections are passionate!

Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)

 Not only is your appreciation of beauty heightened today, but your affection for someone who is “different” or far away is strong. This is the classic day to meet new people under unusual circumstances. It could be sudden. Perhaps the person you’re attracted to is a character?

Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)

 Enjoy schmoozing with friends and groups today because this is a feel-good day. Many relationships will feel flirtatious. If you do feel affectionate toward someone, those feelings will become more passionate. You will also feel selfless in your desire to help those in need.

Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)

 People notice you more than usual today. (In fact, some people know personal details about your private life.) Meanwhile, this is an excellent day to enjoy the company of partners, spouses and close friends because romantic vibes are sweet and special.

Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)

 A work-related romance or flirtation might begin for some of you today. For others, feelings of affection for a co-worker will be mutually rewarding. You might also look for ways to help a co-worker. Others might rescue a pet today.

Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)

 This is a fabulous day for romantic encounters, social outings and special dates. It’s easy for you to feel sensitive to the needs and feelings of loved ones. You’ll also want to indulge yourself today. You might feel inspired by the arts, music or poetry.

Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)

 Today you have a warm feeling in your tummy about family members, which is why you will want to help a relative, especially if they need your assistance. Remember: true generosity is giving what is needed.

If today is your birthday

Actor Channing Tatum (1980) shares your birthday today. You are a bold visionary who pays attention to detail because you like things to be smooth-running and efficient. This is a year of work and building to create solid foundations in your life. Simplicity is key. Take charge of your health. Physical exercise will be important.
news.author: Georgia Nicols

news.title: Timberwolves lose Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo to leg injuries in Game 4 against Nuggets
news.text: MINNEAPOLIS — Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo were injured in the first half of Game 4 of Minnesota’s playoff series against Denver on Saturday night, leaving the Timberwolves without both of their starting guards.
news.author: Patrick Donnelly | ap

news.title: Wolves lose Edwards and DiVincenzo with leg injuries in Game 4
news.text:
MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo were injured in the first half of Game 4 of Minnesota’s playoff series against Denver on Saturday night, leaving the Timberwolves without both of their starting guards.

Edwards’ left knee appeared to buckle as he landed after contesting a layup late in the second quarter. He was helped to the locker room and did not return.

DiVincenzo appeared to injure his lower right leg in the game’s opening minutes when he slipped to the court as he raced to chase a ball. He immediately waved to the bench for help and put little to no weight on his right foot as he was helped to the locker room. The Timberwolves said DiVincenzo would miss the remainder of the game.

Edwards averaged a team-high 23.0 and 8.0 rebounds as the sixth-seeded Timberwolves took a 2-1 lead over Denver. DiVincenzo averaged 14.3 points and made 11 of 22 3-pointers in the first three games.

AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba
news.author: Patrick Donnelly

news.title: Minnesota's Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo forced out of Game 4 with leg injuries
news.text:
This is potentially devastating news for the Timberwolves.

Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo each had to leave Game 4 in Minnesota with first-half leg injuries, and neither will return to the game. 

DiVincenzo has suffered a torn Achilles, reports Shams Charania of ESPN, which is what it appeared to be when it happened. That injury not only ends this season for him but also sidelines him for most, if not all, of next season. DiVincenzo averaged 14.3 points per game in the playoffs and was 11-of-22 from 3-point range through the first three games.

Edwards is still undergoing tests on his left knee injury, and no diagnosis has been made. He appeared to hyperextend his left knee after he went up to challenge a Cameron Johnson shot at the rim.

Edwards had been playing through a case of what the team described as runner’s knee in his right leg, the opposite of the one injured on this play.

Edwards had played in 61 games this season — and did not qualify for postseason awards — due to a variety of injuries. He’d been healthy in the playoffs so far and was averaging 23 points, eight rebounds and four assists a game through the first three games of the series against Denver, leading Minnesota to a 2-1 series lead entering Saturday.
news.author: Kurt Helin

news.title: Anthony Edwards, DiVincenzo Injured, Wolves Beat Nikola Jokic, Nuggets as Fans React to Game 4 Win
news.text:
The Minnesota Timberwolves defeated Nikola Jokic and the Denver Nuggets 112-96 to take a 3-1 lead in their first-round NBA Playoff series on Saturday despite injuries to Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo.

Two key players went down for the Minnesota Timberwolves, as Edwards and DiVincenzo both exited.

DiVincenzo suffered a non-contact injury that resulted in a torn achilles, according to Shams Charania of ESPN. Edwards went down after taking contact at the rim. Neither player returned after being sent to the locker room. 

With just one more win needed to clinch the series, fans are eyeing a 4-1 finish for the Timberwolves in Denver.

Before exiting late in the second quarter, Edwards finished with five points and three rebounds in 18 minutes. Ayo Dosunmu erupted off the bench for 43 points on 13-of-17 shooting, stepping up in Edwards' absence. Julius Randle led the starters with 15 points and nine rebounds.

Despite a combined 54 points from Jokic and Jamal Murray—with Jokic posting a double-double of 24 points and 15 rebounds—the Nuggets could not secure the road win.

The Timberwolves are aiming to break through after back-to-back Western Conference Finals exits and first-round eliminations in the two years prior.

After a 4-1 loss to the Thunder last postseason, Minnesota could be on track for a rematch if both teams continue their current path, with OKC holding a 3-0 edge over the Phoenix Suns.

However, a potential absence of Edwards looms as a major concern.

The winner of the Nuggets-Timberwolves series will advance to face either the No. 2 San Antonio Spurs or the No. 7 Portland Trail Blazers in the Western Conference semifinals, with San Antonio leading 2-1.

Game 5 between Minnesota and the Denver Nuggets is set for Monday as the series shifts back to Denver.
news.author: Taylyn Hadley

news.title: T-wolves beat Nuggets, lose Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo to injuries
news.text:
Ayo Dosunmu came off the bench to score a career-high 43 points on 13-for-17 shooting, and the short-handed Minnesota Timberwolves pulled away for a 112-96 win over the Denver Nuggets in Game 4 of their Western Conference quarterfinals series on Saturday night in Minneapolis.

The victory, which gave Minnesota a 3-1 lead in the best-of-seven series, came at a cost to the Timberwolves, who lost two starters due to injury.

Anthony Edwards, a four-time All-Star and the team's top scorer, left in the second quarter and did not return because of a left knee injury. Edwards leaped to try to block a shot and landed awkwardly on his left leg, and he quickly dropped to the court and clutched his knee.

Team officials helped Edwards to his feet and he placed his arms around their shoulders. He put little weight on his left leg as he hobbled toward the locker room.

Earlier in the first half, Timberwolves guard Donte DiVincenzo headed toward the locker room after he injured his right leg on a non-contact play. DiVincenzo planted his foot to pursue a loose ball and fell to the hardwood, and he grabbed the back of his right leg in pain.

Early reports indicated that DiVincenzo might have ruptured his Achilles tendon on the play.

Naz Reid added 17 points off the bench for Minnesota. Julius Randle finished with 15 points and nine rebounds, and Rudy Gobert grabbed a game-high-tying 15 rebounds to go along with four points.

Jamal Murray scored 30 points on 10-for-25 shooting to lead Denver. Nikola Jokic finished with 24 points, 15 rebounds and nine assists. However, he shot 8-for-22 from the field and missed all three of his 3-point attempts.

The series returns to Denver for Game 5 on Monday night.

Dosunmu took over in his teammates' absence. He helped Minnesota outscore Denver 62-42 in the second half to take a commanding lead in the series.

The Nuggets led 76-74 late in the third quarter before Dosunmu helped Minnesota take charge. He made a pair of free throws as Minnesota finished the quarter on an 8-2 run to grab an 82-78 lead.

In the fourth quarter, Dosunmu continued to assert himself. He hit a driving layup and a pull-up bank shot to increase the Timberwolves' lead to 95-82 with 7:53 to go.

Denver struggled to keep pace the rest of the way, and Jokic took exception to a layup by Jaden McDaniels with 2.1 seconds left. Jokic and Randle were ejected in the closing seconds after players from both teams shoved one another near the sidelines.

--Field Level Media
